title: Tube mounting assembly with radial staking, method of assembly
abstract: The invention provides a novel attachment, staking tool and assembly procedure whereby fluid transfer lines may be secured to an attachment yoke for subsequent assembly to a receiver such as an engine block by radially staking the clamping surface of the yoke about the respective line adjacent the receiver surface.
claims:
Claims We claim: 1. A machine assembly including a clamping yoke of deformable material securing the pilot end of one or more tubular fluid transfer lines to a receiver including a receiver mating surface wherein: (a) at least one fluid transfer line with a main fluid flow axis including an annular collar adjacent its pilot end with a collar axial width extending radially outward to an overall diameter, (b) the clamping yoke includes: i. a clamping surface adjacent said receiver mating surface, ii. an axial through-hole for each of said fluid transfer lines adapted to permit assembly of the yoke to the fluid transfer lines radially, iii. including a through-hole diameter at least as large as the diameter of said fluid transfer line and less than the said overall diameter of the collar, and iv. an through-hole extension passage extending radially of said transfer line with a width at least as large as the diameter of said fluid transfer line and less than the diameter of the collar, (c) the connecting yoke further includes: i. a cylindrical clamping recess on said connecting surface co-axial with said fluid transfer line having: A. a cylindrical outer clamping surface, B. an annular land clamping surface extending radially of the fluid transfer line interior to the yoke, C. a diameter greater than the said overall diameter of said collar, 

 and D. a depth greater than the said axial width of the said collar, ii. at least one inwardly directed staked deformation restraining said collar within said clamping recess, each extending both radially inwardly and axially away from said pilot end to abut the collar and drive the collar against said land surface. 2. A machine assembly including a clamping yoke of deformable material securing the pilot end of one or more tubular fluid transfer lines to a receiver including a receiver mating surface as claimed in claim 1 wherein the spacings between the fluid transfer line at one or more of the line to passage, the collar to recess or the collar and the land to recess and the yoke are a tight sliding fit before or after assembly. 3. A machine assembly including a clamping yoke of deformable material securing the pilot end of one or more tubular fluid transfer lines to a receiver including a receiver mating surface as claimed in claims 1 or 2 wherein the annular collar extends radially outward to a curved outer surface including a maximum diameter within the overall diameter. 4. A machine assembly including a clamping yoke of deformable material securing the pilot end of one or more tubular fluid transfer lines to a receiver including a receiver mating surface as claimed in claim 3 wherein the annular collar includes an operative axial width less than the said collar axial width. 5. A machine assembly including a clamping yoke of deformable material securing the pilot end of one or more tubular fluid transfer lines to a receiver including a receiver mating surface as claimed in claim 4 wherein the annular land clamping surface extends from the fluid transfer line to the maximum diameter. 6. A machine assembly including a clamping yoke of deformable material securing the pilot end of one or more tubular fluid transfer lines to a receiver 

 including a receiver mating surface as claimed in claim 5 wherein the clamping recess depth is less than the axial with of the said collar and greater than the operative axial width. 7. A machine assembly including a clamping yoke of deformable material securing the pilot end of one or more tubular fluid transfer lines to a receiver including a receiver mating surface as claimed in claim 6 wherein the clamping yoke transmits clamping force to the said collar and from the said collar to the receiver. 8. A machine assembly including a clamping yoke of deformable material securing the pilot end of one or more tubular fluid transfer lines to a receiver including a receiver mating surface as claimed in claim 6 wherein the clamping yoke transmits clamping force from the said collar to the receiver mating surface. 9. A machine assembly including a clamping yoke of deformable material securing the pilot end of one or more tubular fluid transfer lines to a receiver including a receiver mating surface as claimed in claim 6 wherein the clamping yoke transmits clamping force to the receiver through the said collar by a spacing between the collar clamping surface and the receiver mating surface. 10. A machine assembly including a clamping yoke of deformable material securing the pilot end of one or more tubular fluid transfer lines to a receiver including a receiver mating surface as claimed in claims 1 through 9 wherein the inwardly directed staked deformation is circular. 11 . A machine assembly including a clamping yoke of deformable material securing the pilot end of one or more tubular fluid transfer lines to a receiver including a receiver mating surface as claimed in claims 1 through 9 wherein the inwardly directed staked deformation forms an incomplete circle on the clamping surface co-axial with the fluid transfer line. 12. A machine assembly including a clamping yoke of deformable material securing the pilot end of one or more tubular fluid transfer lines to a receiver 

 including a receiver mating surface as claimed in claim 11 wherein the incomplete circle is formed by a gap smaller in width than the with of either or both of the diameter of the fluid transfer line or width of the passageway. 13. A machine assembly including a clamping yoke of deformable material securing the pilot end of one or more tubular fluid transfer lines to a receiver including a receiver mating surface as claimed in claim 12 wherein the incomplete circle is formed in two or more continuous circular portions. 14. A machine assembly including a clamping yoke of deformable material securing the pilot end of one or more tubular fluid transfer lines to a receiver including a receiver mating surface as claimed in claims 1 through 13 wherein the inwardly directed staked deformation restrainingly abuts the surface of the collar without penetration and drives against the collar as a surface to surface contact. 15. A machine assembly including a clamping yoke of deformable material securing the pilot end of one or more tubular fluid transfer lines to a receiver including a receiver mating surface as claimed in claim 14 wherein the surface to surface contact includes that part of the collar beyond the maximum diameter in the direction of the pilot end. 16. A machine assembly including a clamping yoke of deformable material securing the pilot end of one or more tubular fluid transfer lines to a receiver including a receiver mating surface as claimed in claim 15 wherein the surface to surface contact includes the bulk of that part of the collar between the maximum diameter and the fluid transfer line in the direction opposite to the direction of the pilot end. 17. A radial staking tool adapted for use in machine assembly of fluid transfer lines to a receiving body including: (a) a blow end, (b) a strike end, (c) a generally cylindrical body co-axial with the direction of a strike 

 extending from the blow end and a body to be struck towards the strike end, (d) the body including an axial gap adapted to accommodate a part upstanding from said body to be struck, and, the blow end including a circular penetration abutment adapted to deform struck material in larger part inwards of the cylindrical body and and in smaller part in the direction of the strike. 18. A radial staking tool adapted for use in machine assembly of fluid transfer lines to a receiving body as claimed in claim 17 wherein the blow end further includes an annular portion transverse to the direction of the strike. 19. A radial staking tool adapted for use in machine assembly of fluid transfer lines to a receiving body as claimed in claim 18 wherein the annular portion is interior to the penetration abutment. 20. A radial staking tool adapted for use in machine assembly of fluid transfer lines to a receiving body as claimed in prior claims 17 or 18 wherein the annular potion is exterior to the penetration abutment. 21 . A radial staking tool adapted for use in machine assembly of fluid transfer lines to a receiving body as claimed in claim 18 wherein the annular portion is both interior to and exterior to the penetration abutment. 22. A procedure for the assembly of fluid transfer lines to a receiving body including assembling at least one fluid transfer line to a transverse connecting yoke as described in any of claims 1 through 16 through the respective through- hole by means of a passageway with a tight sliding fit between between the said fluid transfer line and the yoke, and a) orienting the fluid transfer line about is main fluid-flow axis to a finished angular position, b) radially staking the yoke on its clamping surface with a staking tool as 

 described in any of claims prior 1 -5 and deforming a circular portion of the yoke into the recess and clamping the collar in abutment against the recess, c) securing the assembly to the receiving surface. 23. A procedure for the assembly of fluid transfer lines to a receiving body as claimed in claim 22 wherein said securing drives the receiver mating surface against the collar of the fluid transfer line and the collar against the yoke. 24. A procedure for the assembly of fluid transfer lines to a receiving body as claimed in claim 23 wherein fluid tight sealing is maintained by an O-ring seal between the fluid transfer line and the receiver.
